title: Cisco says critical Webex Services flaw requires customer action
summary: Cisco has released security updates to patch four critical vulnerabilities, including a fixed improper certificate validation flaw in the company's cloud-based Webex Services platform that requires further customer action. [...]

sections.overview: Cisco has released security updates to patch four critical vulnerabilities, including a fixed improper certificate validation flaw in the company's cloud-based Webex Services platform that requires further customer action. [...]

Assessed severity: CRITICAL.
sections.mitigation:
Cisco has released security updates to patch four critical vulnerabilities, including a fixed improper certificate validation flaw in the company's cloud-based Webex Services platform that requires further customer action. [...]

Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls.

email_summary: Cisco has released security updates to patch four critical vulnerabilities, including a fixed improper certificate validation flaw in the company's cloud-based Webex Services platform that requires further customer action. [...]. Severity is rated critical.

title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
summary: Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service.

sections.overview:
Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service. The details of the vulnerabilities are below -

CVE-2026-20184 (CVSS score: 9.8) - An improper certificate validation in the integration of single sign-on (SSO)
sections.technical:
Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service. The details of the vulnerabilities are below -

CVE-2026-20184 (CVSS score: 9.8) - An improper certificate validation in the integration of single sign-on (SSO)

Referenced vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-20184.
sections.impact:
Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service.

Assessed severity: CRITICAL. CVSS score: 9.8/10.
sections.mitigation:
Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service.

Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

email_summary: Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service. Tracked as CVE-2026-20184. Severity is rated critical.

title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
summary: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "crime" part, ancient vulnerabilities somehow still ruining people's days, and enough supply chain drama to fill a season of television nobody asked for.

sections.overview: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "crime" part, ancient vulnerabilities somehow still ruining people's days, and enough supply chain drama to fill a season of television nobody asked for.
sections.technical: A security vulnerability has been identified that could allow attackers to compromise affected systems.
sections.impact:
We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "crime" part, ancient vulnerabilities somehow still ruining people's days, and enough supply chain drama to fill a season of television nobody asked for.

Assessed severity: CRITICAL.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls.
sections.context:
Zero-day vulnerabilities represent the highest-priority threats as they are exploited before vendors can issue patches. Rapid detection and compensating controls are essential.

Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

email_summary: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot. Severity is rated critical.

title: US nationals behind DPRK IT worker 'laptop farm' sent to prison

title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
summary: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.

sections.overview: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.
sections.technical: This involves a ransomware operation, typically leveraging initial access through phishing, exposed services, or supply chain compromise.
sections.impact: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.
sections.mitigation: Ensure offline backups are current and tested. Implement network segmentation and monitor for lateral movement indicators. Affected users should rotate credentials, enable MFA, and monitor accounts for suspicious activity.
sections.context:
Ransomware continues to be one of the most financially impactful categories of cyber threats, with attacks increasingly targeting critical infrastructure and leveraging double-extortion tactics.

Data breaches carry significant regulatory, financial, and reputational consequences. Affected organizations and individuals should monitor for follow-on attacks using exposed data.

email_summary: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.

title: Most "AI SOCs" Are Just Faster Triage. That's Not Enough.
summary: AI-powered SOC tools promise automation, but most only speed up triage instead of reducing real workload. Tines shows how real gains come from end-to-end workflows that execute actions across systems, not just summarize alerts. [...]

title: New ATHR vishing platform uses AI voice agents for automated attacks
summary: A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]

sections.overview: A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]
sections.technical: This involves malicious software designed to compromise, exfiltrate, or disrupt targeted systems. The attack leverages social engineering techniques to trick users into revealing credentials or executing malicious payloads.
sections.impact: A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]
sections.mitigation: Reinforce security awareness training. Ensure email filtering, link scanning, and multi-factor authentication are enabled. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Phishing remains the most common initial access vector. Security awareness training combined with technical controls like email filtering and MFA are key defenses.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

title: UAC-0247 Targets Ukrainian Clinics and Government in Data-Theft Malware Campaign
summary: The Computer Emergencies Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new campaign that has targeted governments and municipal healthcare institutions, mainly clinics and emergency hospitals, to deliver malware capable of stealing sensitive data from Chromium-based web browsers and WhatsApp.

title: Obsidian Plugin Abuse Delivers PHANTOMPULSE RAT in Targeted Finance, Crypto Attacks
summary: A "novel" social engineering campaign has been observed abusing Obsidian, a cross-platform note-taking application, as an initial access vector to distribute a previously undocumented Windows remote access trojan called PHANTOMPULSE in attacks targeting individuals in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors.

sections.overview: A "novel" social engineering campaign has been observed abusing Obsidian, a cross-platform note-taking application, as an initial access vector to distribute a previously undocumented Windows remote access trojan called PHANTOMPULSE in attacks targeting individuals in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors. Dubbed REF6598 by Elastic Security Labs, the activity has been found to leverage
sections.technical: A "novel" social engineering campaign has been observed abusing Obsidian, a cross-platform note-taking application, as an initial access vector to distribute a previously undocumented Windows remote access trojan called PHANTOMPULSE in attacks targeting individuals in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors.

sections.mitigation: Reinforce security awareness training. Ensure email filtering, link scanning, and multi-factor authentication are enabled. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Phishing remains the most common initial access vector. Security awareness training combined with technical controls like email filtering and MFA are key defenses.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

Dubbed REF6598 by Elastic Security Labs, the activity has been found to leverage.

title: Hidden Passenger? How Taboola Routes Logged-In Banking Sessions to Temu
summary: A bank approved a Taboola pixel. That pixel quietly redirected logged-in users to a Temu tracking endpoint. This occurred without the bank’s knowledge, without user consent, and without a single security control registering a violation.

sections.overview: A bank approved a Taboola pixel. That pixel quietly redirected logged-in users to a Temu tracking endpoint. This occurred without the bank’s knowledge, without user consent, and without a single security control registering a violation.

title: [Webinar] Find and Eliminate Orphaned Non-Human Identities in Your Environment
summary: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching.

sections.overview: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching.
sections.technical: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. For every employee in your org, there are 40 to 50 automated credentials: service accounts, API tokens, AI agent connections, andOAuth grants.
sections.impact: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. For every employee in your org, there are 40 to 50 automated credentials: service accounts, API tokens, AI agent connections, andOAuth grants.
sections.mitigation: Reinforce security awareness training. Ensure email filtering, link scanning, and multi-factor authentication are enabled. Affected users should rotate credentials, enable MFA, and monitor accounts for suspicious activity.
sections.context:
Phishing remains the most common initial access vector. Security awareness training combined with technical controls like email filtering and MFA are key defenses.

Data breaches carry significant regulatory, financial, and reputational consequences. Affected organizations and individuals should monitor for follow-on attacks using exposed data.

email_summary: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not weak passwords.
